title: Ayat al-Kursi — The Throne Verse
arabic: اللَّهُ لَا إِلَٰهَ إِلَّا هُوَ الْحَيُّ الْقَيُّومُ
translation: Allah — there is no deity except Him, the Ever-Living, the Sustainer of all existence.
source: Sample lesson — replace with your QQ lesson notes
takeaways: It is verse 255 of Surah al-Baqarah, known as the Throne Verse (Ayat al-Kursi).
In a hadith recorded by Muslim, the Prophet ﷺ described it as the greatest verse in the Quran.
It opens by affirming tawhid: Allah alone, the Ever-Living (al-Hayy) and the Sustainer (al-Qayyum).
reflection: Reciting it after each prayer is a gentle anchor — a moment to remember Who you just stood before.

selfTest.question: Which two of Allah's names open Ayat al-Kursi, and what do they mean?
selfTest.answer: Al-Hayy (the Ever-Living) and al-Qayyum (the Sustainer of all existence).
